audit_summary.verdict: Mobile experience is structurally broken. Sidebar never collapses visually on mobile viewports despite having lg: breakpoint code. Multiple grid layouts use fixed column counts without mobile fallbacks. Touch targets are undersized. The platform feels like a desktop app accidentally viewed on a phone — NOT a mobile-first cognition platform.

global_layout_issues.issue: Sidebar renders at full 256px width on mobile despite having lg:translate-x-0 / -translate-x-full CSS. The sidebar IS hidden by default (translate-x-full) but main content area uses lg:ml-64 margin which does NOT apply at mobile — content starts at left edge. However the sidebar toggle hamburger menu IS present. Core problem: content area has no max-width constraint and renders at desktop proportions inside mobile viewport, causing horizontal scroll or tiny text.
global_layout_issues.why_it_matters: Every authenticated page is affected. Content is unreadably small on phones.

global_layout_issues.issue: No overflow-x:hidden on body or root container. Grids with fixed column counts (grid-cols-2, grid-cols-3, grid-cols-4, grid-cols-7) without mobile breakpoints cause horizontal overflow, enabling horizontal scroll on every page with data cards.
global_layout_issues.why_it_matters: Horizontal scroll on mobile destroys trust and feels broken.

global_layout_issues.issue: Header shows full email 'andre@thestrategysquad.com.au' at all widths. At 390px this overflows or compresses other header elements. Email text is hidden at sm: but the avatar+dropdown still takes significant space.
global_layout_issues.why_it_matters: Header overflow pushes notification icons off-screen on small devices.

navigation_issues.issue: No bottom navigation bar. Mobile users must open hamburger menu for every page switch. Industry standard for mobile apps is persistent bottom nav with 4-5 core actions.
navigation_issues.why_it_matters: Increases navigation friction by 3-4 taps per page transition. Users will abandon.

navigation_issues.issue: Sidebar drawer opens with w-64 (256px) on a 390px screen — takes 65% of viewport. Combined with the backdrop overlay, usable content area during nav is only 134px. Section headers (INTELLIGENCE, EXECUTION, SYSTEMS, GOVERNANCE) with chevron toggles are very small touch targets.
navigation_issues.why_it_matters: Navigation feels cramped. Expandable sections are hard to tap accurately.

navigation_issues.issue: Floating soundboard button is fixed bottom-6 right-6 with w-14 h-14. On mobile this overlaps content and cannot be dismissed. When expanded, the panel is max-w-[calc(100vw-48px)] which is good, but at 390px it leaves only 24px margins — very tight for a chat interface with text input.
navigation_issues.why_it_matters: Soundboard button covers bottom-right content including scroll indicators and CTA buttons.

calibration_flow_issues.issue: Ignition animation renders at full width. The animated orb and text are centered but at 390px the text 'Welcome to BIQc' heading and subtitle may overflow if font-size is not clamped. Uses text-3xl sm:text-4xl which is acceptable.
calibration_flow_issues.why_it_matters: First impression for new users on mobile.

calibration_flow_issues.issue: Form uses max-w-md mx-auto which is fine for mobile. However the social handles section uses grid-cols-2 without mobile fallback — at 390px with px-4 margins, each social handle input gets ~155px width which is tight for URLs.
calibration_flow_issues.why_it_matters: Text input for social URLs is cramped on mobile.

calibration_flow_issues.issue: WOW Summary fields use max-w-2xl (672px) with px-4 sm:px-8. At 390px, effective content width is 358px. Individual field cards render fine (full width). Edit pencil icon and 'Click to edit' text work on touch. Button is full-width and touchable. ACCEPTABLE.
calibration_flow_issues.why_it_matters: This flow actually works reasonably well on mobile.

calibration_flow_issues.issue: Identity bar shows email, Admin buttons (Back, Skip), and Sign out — all in a single row with px-5. At 390px these buttons wrap or overflow. Touch targets for 'Skip (Admin)' and 'Back' buttons are 24px height — below 44px minimum.
calibration_flow_issues.why_it_matters: Admin controls are unusable on mobile. Buttons too small to tap.

dashboard_issues.component: Cognition tabs (Money, Revenue, Operations, People, Market)
dashboard_issues.issue: Tabs use flex gap-2 overflow-x-auto with shrink-0 on each tab. Each tab is ~120px wide. 5 tabs = 600px minimum. At 390px (minus sidebar margin) only 2.5 tabs visible. Horizontal scroll is enabled but there is NO scroll indicator — users don't know more tabs exist. Tab text uses font-semibold text-sm which is acceptable.
dashboard_issues.why_it_matters: Users will only see Money and Revenue tabs. Market tab (most important) is hidden off-screen with no visual indicator.

dashboard_issues.issue: Resolution items use rounded-2xl p-5 with action buttons inside. Action buttons (Auto-Email, Quick-SMS, Hand Off, Complete, Ignore) use flex flex-wrap gap-2 which handles mobile well. However each button is only ~80px × 30px — below 44px touch target minimum.
dashboard_issues.why_it_matters: Action buttons are too small to tap accurately on touch screens.

dashboard_issues.issue: Sticky header is fixed at top-14 with flex items-center justify-between. At 390px, the status text (DRIFT), velocity indicator, and confidence badge all compete for space. Wrapping is handled by flex-wrap but the result is a multi-line header that pushes content down.
dashboard_issues.why_it_matters: Status bar takes excessive vertical space on mobile, reducing visible content area.

dashboard_issues.issue: Weekly brief shows 4 metrics ($recovered, hours saved, actions, SOP%) in a flex row with sm:gap-6 gap-1. At 390px the gap-1 makes metrics merge visually. Each metric label uses text-[9px] which is below readable threshold on Android.
dashboard_issues.why_it_matters: Key performance metrics are unreadable on mobile.

market_tab_issues.issue: Signal scores use grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-4. At 390px this renders 2 columns which is CORRECT. Each card gets ~175px width. Text inside uses text-lg font-bold with JetBrains Mono — long values like 'Moderate interest in consulting services' overflow the card width and wrap to 3+ lines.
market_tab_issues.why_it_matters: Signal card text overflow creates uneven card heights and broken layout.

market_tab_issues.issue: Drift cards use grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-4. At 390px, 2 columns. Each drift card shows percentage (45%/70%) with progress bar and gap indicator. The text-xl font-bold numbers and text-xs gap labels render correctly at 2-col. ACCEPTABLE but tight.
market_tab_issues.why_it_matters: Drift visualization works at 2-col but cards are compressed.

market_tab_issues.issue: Channel cards use grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-3. At 390px, 2 columns. Each channel card has a 40px icon + name + description + status badge. At 175px width per card, the description text (e.g., 'Mailchimp, ActiveCampaign') wraps to multiple lines. Channel name + provider text may truncate.
market_tab_issues.why_it_matters: Channel cards are too compressed at 2-col on mobile. Should be 1 column.

market_tab_issues.issue: Uses grid-cols-3 md:grid-cols-7 gap-2. At 390px, 3 columns for 7 metric placeholders (CAC, CPL, ROAS, Conv Rate, Dependency, Drop-off, Saturation). Each metric cell is ~115px wide. Labels like 'Dependency' and 'Saturation' at text-[9px] are at the readability limit.
market_tab_issues.why_it_matters: Metric labels are unreadable on small screens.

market_tab_issues.issue: Action Plan probability cards use grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3. At 390px, single column — CORRECT. Marketing moves list uses full width — CORRECT. Blindside risk and Hidden lever use grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2. At 390px, single column — CORRECT. 90-day projection uses grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3. At 390px, single column — CORRECT. Action Plan mobile layout is WELL DESIGNED.
market_tab_issues.why_it_matters: Action Plan is the best mobile-designed section. No issues.

market_tab_issues.issue: Strategic Alignment Check uses grid-cols-2 gap-3 for Misalignment Index and Goal Achievement — NO mobile breakpoint. At 390px each card is ~175px. The text-2xl bold numbers display fine but cards are tight.
market_tab_issues.why_it_matters: Should be grid-cols-1 on mobile for better readability.

insights_tabs_issues.issue: Tab content renders at max-w-5xl (1024px) with px-6. At 390px, content is 378px wide which is fine. Individual resolution items and insight cards render at full width. The primary issue is the TAB BAR itself (see DASH-001) — tabs scroll horizontally with no indicator.
insights_tabs_issues.why_it_matters: Content within tabs is acceptable. Tab selection UX is broken.

integration_pages_issues.issue: Merge.dev OAuth linking opens in a popup window. On Android Chrome, popup windows are often blocked or open in new tabs. The redirect back to the app after OAuth may not restore the original tab state.
integration_pages_issues.why_it_matters: Integration connection flow may fail silently on mobile browsers.

integration_pages_issues.issue: Question options use rounded-xl px-6 py-5 with flex items-center gap-3. At 390px, each option gets full width minus px-6 padding = 342px. The radio circle (w-5 h-5) and option text render well. Continue/Back buttons are full-width. Progress bar renders correctly. ACCEPTABLE mobile experience.
integration_pages_issues.why_it_matters: Forensic calibration is well-designed for mobile.

admin_panel_issues.issue: Admin dashboard uses grid-cols-2 for user stat cards without mobile fallback. User list table renders at full width with horizontal scroll. Admin actions (suspend, impersonate) have small touch targets.
admin_panel_issues.why_it_matters: Admin panel is rarely used on mobile but should still be functional.

animation_performance_issues.issue: Loading animation uses framer-motion with multiple animated elements (orb, dots, progress bar, text transitions). On mid-range Android devices (Snapdragon 600 series), this can cause frame drops. The animation runs at page center with full-screen overlay. No reduced-motion media query detected.
animation_performance_issues.why_it_matters: Animation performance is critical for perceived quality. Frame drops = broken feel.

animation_performance_issues.issue: CognitiveMesh uses CSS keyframe animations (opacity, transform). These are GPU-accelerated and should perform well on mobile. RadarSweep uses similar approach. No requestAnimationFrame or heavy JS animations detected. ACCEPTABLE performance profile.
animation_performance_issues.why_it_matters: Loading animations are CSS-based and performant.

animation_performance_issues.issue: No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) queries detected anywhere in the codebase. Users with motion sensitivity settings enabled will still see all animations.
animation_performance_issues.why_it_matters: Accessibility compliance. Required by WCAG 2.1 Level AA.

font_rendering_issues.issue: Multiple elements use text-[9px] and text-[10px] with JetBrains Mono font. At these sizes on Android, JetBrains Mono is barely legible due to its wide character spacing. Elements affected: channel descriptions, metric labels, source badges, KPI labels, gap percentages.
font_rendering_issues.why_it_matters: Sub-11px monospace text is unreadable on mobile screens.

font_rendering_issues.issue: Heading font Cormorant Garamond renders thin on Android compared to iOS. At text-2xl (24px) it looks elegant. At text-sm (14px) used for some card titles, the thin weight becomes hard to read against the dark background.
font_rendering_issues.why_it_matters: Serif fonts at small sizes on Android have poor rendering.

state_transition_issues.issue: Snapshot fetch calls Edge Function which can take 10-15 seconds on cold start. During this time, CognitiveLoadingScreen shows. On mobile networks (3G/4G), combined with Supabase Edge Function cold start, total wait can be 20+ seconds. No timeout or fallback to cached data within the loading component itself.
state_transition_issues.why_it_matters: 20-second loading on mobile is unacceptable. Users will close the app.

state_transition_issues.issue: Market page has an 8-second timeout race but tries 3 sequential API calls (snapshot/latest, market-intelligence, Edge Function). On mobile networks, these can each take 3-5 seconds, totaling 9-15 seconds. The 8-second timeout may fire before the fallback Edge Function call.
state_transition_issues.why_it_matters: Page may render without data even when data is available, just slow.

authentication_issues.issue: Login page uses min-h-screen flex with two panels. Right panel has hidden lg:flex which correctly hides on mobile. Left panel uses px-8 md:px-16 which gives adequate mobile padding. Sign in button is full-width and tappable. OAuth buttons (Google, Microsoft) are full-width. ACCEPTABLE.

Auth callback page shows 'Completing sign in...' with status text. On mobile, if the OAuth popup redirects to a new tab, the callback page may load in the popup tab instead of the original tab. User may end up with two tabs — one stuck on callback, one on login.
authentication_issues.why_it_matters: OAuth flow on mobile browsers is unreliable with popup-based redirects.

authentication_issues.issue: Auth bootstrap re-triggers on session token refresh, causing component remount. On mobile with unstable network, token refresh can happen frequently, causing the loading screen to flash repeatedly. The lastBootstrapUserId ref prevents re-bootstrap for the same user but doesn't prevent the initial loading flash.
authentication_issues.why_it_matters: Repeated loading flashes on mobile degrade perceived performance.

keyboard_input_issues.issue: Input field does not have inputMode='url' attribute. Android keyboard will show standard QWERTY instead of URL-optimized keyboard with .com shortcut. Input type is 'url' which is correct but inputMode adds additional mobile optimization.
keyboard_input_issues.why_it_matters: Users must manually type URLs without keyboard shortcuts.

keyboard_input_issues.issue: When keyboard opens on mobile, the fixed-position soundboard panel doesn't adjust. The input field may be pushed behind the keyboard. No handling for visual viewport resize or keyboard events.
keyboard_input_issues.why_it_matters: Users cannot see what they're typing in the soundboard on mobile.

safe_area_issues.issue: viewport meta has viewport-fit=cover but no env(safe-area-inset-*) CSS detected. On Android devices with navigation gestures or notched displays, bottom content may be obscured by the system navigation bar.
safe_area_issues.why_it_matters: Bottom-positioned elements (Soundboard button, action buttons) may be partially hidden.

accessibility_issues.issue: Multiple interactive elements are below the 44×44px minimum touch target: action buttons (Auto-Email ~80x30px), tab badges, source tags, dismiss buttons, admin controls. Minimum recommended by WCAG is 44x44px for touch targets.
accessibility_issues.why_it_matters: Users with motor impairments or large fingers will mis-tap frequently.

accessibility_issues.issue: Text color #64748B on background #0F1720 has a contrast ratio of approximately 3.8:1, below the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text. This affects all label text, descriptions, and secondary content across the platform.
accessibility_issues.why_it_matters: Legal accessibility requirement. Low contrast text is unreadable in bright outdoor conditions.

performance_observations.observation: Edge Function cold start on mobile 4G adds 8-15 seconds to first meaningful paint on /advisor and /market pages. Warmup ping exists but only fires on app load — if user navigates directly to /market, the Edge Function may be cold.
performance_observations.impact: First load on mobile can take 20+ seconds

performance_observations.observation: Market page makes 3 sequential API calls (snapshot/latest, market-intelligence, Edge Function) before rendering content. On mobile, these should be parallelized or the page should render progressively as each call returns.
performance_observations.impact: Unnecessary sequential latency on mobile networks

performance_observations.observation: DashboardLayout imports 20+ Lucide icons at the top level. Tree-shaking should handle this but worth verifying bundle size impact.

recommended_fixes_prioritized.title: Add mobile grid breakpoints to ALL grid-cols-2+ without sm: fallback
recommended_fixes_prioritized.affected: MarketPage, AdvisorWatchtower, Settings, OnboardingWizard, HomePage, AdminDashboard, AlertsPageAuth, ForensicCalibration results
recommended_fixes_prioritized.fix: Replace grid-cols-2 with grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2. Replace grid-cols-3 with grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-3. Replace grid-cols-4 with grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-4. Replace grid-cols-7 with grid-cols-3 sm:grid-cols-4 md:grid-cols-7.

recommended_fixes_prioritized.title: Add overflow-x-hidden to root container
recommended_fixes_prioritized.fix: Add overflow-x: hidden to the body element or the root DashboardLayout container to prevent horizontal scroll from any overflowing grid.

recommended_fixes_prioritized.title: Increase touch targets to minimum 44px
recommended_fixes_prioritized.affected: Action buttons, tab badges, admin controls, dismiss buttons
recommended_fixes_prioritized.fix: Add min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px] to all interactive elements. For inline buttons, use padding to achieve minimum touch area. For the action bar buttons specifically: change py-1.5 to py-2.5 and px-3 to px-4.

recommended_fixes_prioritized.title: Add scroll indicator to cognition tabs
recommended_fixes_prioritized.fix: Add a gradient fade-out on the right edge of the tab bar to indicate more tabs exist. Or add a horizontal scroll indicator dots below the tabs. CSS: mask-image: linear-gradient(to right, black 85%, transparent).

recommended_fixes_prioritized.title: Increase minimum font size to 11px on mobile
recommended_fixes_prioritized.fix: Replace text-[9px] with text-[11px] sm:text-[9px] and text-[10px] with text-[11px] sm:text-[10px] for JetBrains Mono labels. This increases readability on mobile while preserving desktop density.

recommended_fixes_prioritized.title: Parallelize Market page API calls
recommended_fixes_prioritized.fix: Change fetchSnapshot to call all 3 sources (snapshot/latest, market-intelligence, Edge Function) with Promise.allSettled instead of sequential try/catch. Use the first successful response.

recommended_fixes_prioritized.title: Add prefers-reduced-motion support
recommended_fixes_prioritized.affected: CognitiveLoadingScreen, CognitiveMesh, RadarSweep, all CSS animations
recommended_fixes_prioritized.fix: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; } } to global CSS.

recommended_fixes_prioritized.title: Add safe-area-inset padding for bottom elements
recommended_fixes_prioritized.fix: Add padding-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom) to fixed bottom elements. For soundboard: bottom: calc(1.5rem + env(safe-area-inset-bottom)).

recommended_fixes_prioritized.title: Fix soundboard keyboard overlay on mobile
recommended_fixes_prioritized.fix: Use visual viewport API to detect keyboard and adjust panel position. Or change from fixed positioning to a full-screen modal on mobile that properly handles keyboard resize.

recommended_fixes_prioritized.title: Add bottom navigation bar for mobile
recommended_fixes_prioritized.fix: Add a fixed bottom nav bar (visible only at <lg breakpoint) with 5 core actions: Insights, Market, Revenue, Integrations, Settings. Hide when keyboard is open. Apply safe-area-inset-bottom padding.

recommended_fixes_prioritized.title: Improve color contrast for secondary text
recommended_fixes_prioritized.fix: Change secondary text color from #64748B to #8494A7 (contrast ratio 5.2:1) to meet WCAG AA compliance while maintaining the Liquid Steel aesthetic.

recommended_fixes_prioritized.title: Calibration identity bar mobile layout
recommended_fixes_prioritized.fix: Stack admin controls vertically on mobile or hide non-essential controls. Increase button padding to meet 44px touch target. Use flex-wrap with gap-2.
